note: be-test: partial write be=0xC (R byte only effective)
be-test: readback after partial write be=0xC
be-test: lookup after be=0xC write → G=0xCC B=0xDD unchanged
be-test: partial write be=0x2 (G byte only)
be-test: readback after partial write be=0x2
be-test: lookup after be=0x2 write → G=0x77, R and B unchanged
be-test: partial write be=0x1 (B byte only)
be-test: readback after partial write be=0x1
be-test: lookup after be=0x1 write → B=0x88, R and G unchanged
overwrite first write R=0xFF G=0 B=0
overwrite second write R=0 G=0xFF B=0
blend9a dst white (should be zeroed out)
blend9a sb=8 db=0 → output = src exactly
blend9b sb=0 db=8 → output = dst exactly (R=0x80 G=0x40 B=0xFF)
blend11a do_blend=0 → output=src regardless of dst and coefficients
